// Fetch loan data from database or API
// This is a mock implementation - replace with actual API/database call
const loans = [
  {
    loanId: 'LN-2024-001',
    memberId: 'M-1001',
    memberName: 'John Doe',
    loanAmount: 500000,
    outstandingBalance: 350000,
    interestRate: 12.5,
    installmentAmount: 25000,
    dueDate: '2024-06-15',
    lastPaymentDate: '2024-05-10',
    daysPastDue: 0,
    debtToIncomeRatio: 38,
    loanPurpose: 'Business Expansion',
    collateralValue: 750000,
    riskCategory: 'Low',
    status: 'Active'
  },
  {
    loanId: 'LN-2024-002',
    memberId: 'M-1002',
    memberName: 'Jane Smith',
    loanAmount: 300000,
    outstandingBalance: 295000,
    interestRate: 14.0,
    installmentAmount: 15000,
    dueDate: '2024-05-25',
    lastPaymentDate: '2024-04-25',
    daysPastDue: 21,
    debtToIncomeRatio: 52,
    loanPurpose: 'Medical Emergency',
    collateralValue: 400000,
    riskCategory: 'Medium',
    status: 'Active'
  },
  {
    loanId: 'LN-2024-003',
    memberId: 'M-1003',
    memberName: 'Robert Johnson',
    loanAmount: 1000000,
    outstandingBalance: 980000,
    interestRate: 15.5,
    installmentAmount: 45000,
    dueDate: '2024-04-10',
    lastPaymentDate: '2024-03-10',
    daysPastDue: 62,
    debtToIncomeRatio: 67,
    loanPurpose: 'Real Estate',
    collateralValue: 1200000,
    riskCategory: 'High',
    status: 'Active'
  }
];

// Add timestamp for monitoring run
const monitoringRun = {
  timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
  totalLoans: loans.length,
  loans: loans
};

return [
  {
    json: monitoringRun
  }
];
